component_identification.content: Using the official rulebook text as the primary source, set up Photosynthesis by first sorting all components into player materials, shared components, randomizers, and state markers. Verify component names and iconography against the rulebook contents section before anyone chooses a role or starts the first turn. This avoids the most common setup failure: missing or mis-sorted decks/tokens that break later rules interactions. The rulebook references these core pieces repeatedly in setup and play: Game Components ӼӼ Illustrated Rules ӼӼ 1 Game Board ӼӼ 1 Sun Segment ӼӼ 4 Sun Revolution Counters ӼӼ 4 Player Boards ӼӼ 1 First Player Token ӼӼ 24 Scoring Tokens ӼӼ 4 Light Point Trackers ӼӼ 24 Seed Tokens (6 of each variety) ӼӼ 32 Small Trees (8 of each variety) ӼӼ 16 Medium Trees (4 of each variety) ӼӼ 8 Large Trees (2 of each variety) O bject of the game Using your light points, take your trees through their life cycles, from seedlings to fully grown. ÊÊ Growing from a Seed to a Small ...

core_game_loop.content: The official rules describe a repeatable turn rhythm for Photosynthesis: perform start-of-turn checks, execute the game’s main action structure in the stated order, resolve triggered effects, then complete cleanup/end-step timing before passing play. Teach this as a fixed checklist so new players do not skip mandatory windows. If an interrupt or reaction occurs, resolve it completely, then return to the unresolved step. Rulebook turn-sequence cues include: Reminder: All of the above actions, except buying, activate a space on the main board (meaning you cannot act upon it again during your turn).This means that during your turn you cannot, for example, plant a seed and then grow it, grow a tree and then use it as a point of origin for a seed, use a tree as a point of origin for a seed and then grow it, or collect a tree and plant a seed in its space. The Life ...

detailed_rules.content: Detailed play should follow a strict rulebook hierarchy: explicit card/effect text first, scenario-specific instructions second, and baseline turn structure third. In Photosynthesis, most disputes come from timing windows, target legality, replacement effects, and whether an action is optional or mandatory. Read 'must', 'may', and 'cannot' literally; these terms define obligations and restrictions. Always verify eligibility before paying costs, then resolve effects sentence by sentence. If an effect changes board state mid-resolution, finish the current instruction chain before opening new optional actions unless the text says otherwise. Important operational clauses extracted from the official rulebook include: ÊÊ Growing from a Seed to a Small Tree costs 1 light point ÊÊ Growing from a Small Tree to a Medium Tree costs 2 light points ÊÊ Growing from a Medium Tree to a Large Tree costs 3 light points Players can only take new trees from their available area to grow seeds or trees on the main board.Trees and seeds that are removed and replaced from the main board are then placed on the Player Board, on the topmost available space. When the sun has completed one revolution around the board, the top token of the Sun Revolution Counter pile is drawn and placed in the box. Reminder: All of the above actions, except buying, activate a space on the main board (meaning you cannot act upon it again during your turn).This means that during your turn you cannot, for example, plant a seed and then grow it, grow a tree and then use it as a point of origin for a seed, ...

scoring_and_endgame.content: Scoring and endgame are determined only by printed rulebook conditions. During play, track all score/objective progress continuously so players can identify whether the game ends immediately on trigger or after round completion. When an end trigger occurs, resolve pending mandatory effects in the order defined by the rules, then apply final scoring and tie-breakers exactly as written. Official scoring references include: Light points are scored as follows: ÊÊ A Small Tree scores 1 point ÊÊ A Medium Tree scores 2 points ÊÊ A Large Tree scores 3 points No points are scored for seedlings. ÊÊ Growing from a Seed to a Small Tree costs 1 light point ÊÊ Growing from a Small Tree to a Medium Tree costs 2 light points ÊÊ Growing from a Medium Tree to a Large Tree costs 3 light points Players can only take new trees from their available area to grow seeds or trees on the main board.Trees and seeds that are removed and ...

beginner_strategy.content: Beginner strategy from the official rules for Photosynthesis: (1) Prioritize legal, repeatable value every turn before attempting high-variance combos. (2) Plan around timing windows—many beginner mistakes are not tactical, they are sequencing errors. (3) Preserve flexibility by keeping at least one adaptable action/resource each round, so you can respond to interrupts and shifting board states. (4) Study loss conditions and penalties early; defensive rule awareness prevents avoidable collapses. (5) In the endgame, choose objective-completing actions over cosmetic optimization, because trigger timing usually matters more than one extra short-term gain. These recommendations come directly from the turn structure and win-condition logic in the official rulebook text.
